message:
fix(workers): inline Worker URL construction to prevent bundler hoisting

Changes all Worker instantiation from:
  const workerUrl = new URL('./worker.ts', import.meta.url);
  worker = new Worker(workerUrl, { type: 'module' });

To:
  worker = new Worker(new URL('./worker.ts', import.meta.url), { type: 'module' });

**Why This Matters:**

Modern bundlers (Vite, esbuild, Rollup) detect `new Worker(new URL(...))` as
a special pattern and handle worker bundling correctly. When the URL is
extracted to a variable first, some bundlers may:

1. Hoist the URL construction to module scope
2. Resolve the path incorrectly in production builds
3. Fail to include the worker in the bundle manifest

By inlining the URL construction directly in the Worker constructor call,
we ensure the bundler can reliably detect and process worker imports.

**Files Changed (18 total):**

Overlay Components (14 files):
- AnomalyOverlay.tsx, CGROverlay.tsx, CRISPROverlay.tsx
- CommandPalette.tsx, ComparisonOverlay.tsx, DotPlotOverlay.tsx
- EpistasisOverlay.tsx, HGTOverlay.tsx, HilbertOverlay.tsx
- ModuleOverlay.tsx, RNAStructureOverlay.tsx, SearchOverlay.tsx
- SyntenyOverlay.tsx, OverlayProvider.tsx

Database & Workers:
- DatabaseLoader.ts (gzip-decompress.worker.ts)
- ComputeOrchestrator.ts (analysis.worker.ts, simulation.worker.ts)
- WorkerPreloader.ts (search.worker.ts)

Mobile Components:
- PhagePickerSheet.tsx (search.worker.ts)
  - Also adds smart auto-focus behavior: skips keyboard popup on touch
    devices to prevent disruptive viewport shifts. Detects touch via
    ontouchstart, maxTouchPoints, pointer:coarse, hover:none, and
    viewport width <= 768px.

All worker instantiations maintain the module/classic worker fallback
pattern for browser compatibility:
  try { worker = new Worker(url, { type: 'module' }); }
  catch { worker = new Worker(url); }

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
